FIX-001.verification: Header.tsx wraps inner blocks in <div className='container'>. Header.php wraps $children in <div class='container'>. Page audit confirms all 7 .header sections have .container found=true.

FIX-002.verification: HeaderMainItem.tsx line 174 includes 'text-white' class on submenu container. HeaderMainItem.php line 68 includes 'text-white' class. Text is now visible against charcoal background.

FIX-003.issue: Logo should span full header height
FIX-003.verification: Both TSX (lines 588, 599) and PHP (line 60) use 'self-stretch **:h-full' instead of previous 'self-center **:h-6.5 md:**:h-14'. Logo now fills available nav bar height.

FIX-004.issue: Search should be a form with input and submit button
FIX-004.verification: Both TSX and PHP render <form role='search'> with <input type='search'> and <button type='submit'> with SearchIcon. Desktop: 250px in utility row. Mobile: full-width. Page audit confirms search_form found=true, count=2 on all 6 content sections (desktop + mobile forms).

FIX-005.issue: SVG asset paths broken in PHP
FIX-005.verification: All theme_block_asset() calls in HeaderMain.php use paths without 'resources/' prefix (ExternalArrow.svg, SearchIcon.svg, MainMenuOpen.svg, MainMenuClose.svg). HeaderMainItem.php uses dirname(__DIR__) for ChevronDown.svg. Page audit confirms: external_arrow_svg found on sections 0,2 (those with SEP link), mobile_toggle found on all 6 sections.

FIX-006.issue: SEP renamed to Call to Action
FIX-006.verification: TSX PanelBody title is 'Call to Action' (line 357). Placeholder text is 'Call to Action' (line 375-378). Fallback display text is 'Call to Action' (line 534, 673-676). No remaining references to 'SEP Link' in the TSX UI labels.

FIX-007.verification: Panel titles updated: 'Logo Settings', 'Call to Action', 'Search', 'Mobile CTA', 'Behaviour'. BaseControls have id props: header-logo-control, header-sep-link, header-search-link, header-mobile-cta-link. Descriptive labels added for CTA and Search.

FIX-008.verification: Root cause was same as FIX-005 (SVG path issue). Page audit confirms mobile_toggle button found on all 6 content sections. Hamburger SVG renders correctly.

FIX-009.verification: Developer RE-ADDED @custom-variant not-menu-open to states.css (line 5). This was EXPLICITLY removed in the previous fix cycle per user instruction. The user stated this variant should NOT exist. The code should use default-state approach (collapsed by default, menu-open variant expands). HeaderMain.php line 130 uses not-menu-open:max-md:max-h-0, not-menu-open:max-md:opacity-0, not-menu-open:max-md:pointer-events-none. These must be replaced with default classes.

FQA-R001.verification: Previous run found 1 prettier error in HeaderMainItem.tsx:57. Now there are 7 prettier/prettier errors in HeaderMain.tsx (lines 362, 375, 389, 562, 606, 614, 623, 630) plus the original. The new fixes introduced additional formatting inconsistencies.

FQA-R002.verification: Header.tsx line 22 still destructures clientId but never uses it. sortBlocks on line 23 does not take clientId as argument.

FQA-R003.verification: BaseControls for Logo, CTA, Search, and Mobile CTA now have id props. However, the two BaseControls in the Behaviour panel (Desktop at line 435 and Mobile at line 468) still have label without id prop.

build.notes: Build compiles successfully. 3 pre-existing size warnings (editor.js 301 KiB, entrypoint sizes). No TypeScript or compilation errors.

lint.notes: Multiple lint errors found. 8 prettier/prettier formatting errors in HeaderMain.tsx. 1 unused variable (clientId in Header.tsx). 2 BaseControl-without-id errors in HeaderMain.tsx (lines 435, 468). 3 unused imports in Header.tsx (BlockControls, ToolbarButton, ToolbarGroup -- wait, these are in HeaderMain.tsx not Header.tsx). Let me verify.
headerBlockIssues: Header.tsx:22 - clientId is defined but never used (@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars)
HeaderMain.tsx:435 - BaseControl with label but no id
HeaderMain.tsx:468 - BaseControl with label but no id
HeaderMain.tsx:562 - prettier/prettier: 'mid' should not be quoted

testPageVerification.notes: Test page contains 6 takt/header block instances in raw content. All blocks render on frontend. Real media (logoId: 6) used in all variations.

registration.notes: All 4 blocks registered correctly. Header (takt/header) with multiple:false. HeaderMain (takt/header-main) parent:takt/header, multiple:false. HeaderMainItem (takt/header-main-item) parent:takt/header-main. HeaderMainSubItem (takt/header-main-sub-item) parent:takt/header-main-item.

attributes.notes: All spec-required attributes present in block.json files with correct types and defaults. Scroll behavior data attributes confirmed on all variations via page audit.

accessibility.notes: Mobile toggle has aria-controls, aria-expanded, aria-label in both TSX and PHP. Logo link has aria-label. Nav element has aria-label='Main Navigation' and tabindex=-1. Submenu buttons have aria-haspopup, aria-expanded, aria-controls. SubItems have role=menuitem. JS implements focus trapping, Escape key, ArrowUp/Down keyboard navigation, inert attribute management.

markupDuplication.notes: No duplication. $children rendered once in HeaderMain.php (line 139). Single button + single submenu per nav item. Page audit confirms: Variation 1 (Full Content) has 16 links in header-main, 17 total in header wrapper (includes SEP mobile pill). SEP mobile pill is the documented acceptable exception.

tsxPhpSync.notes: Critical sync issue: PHP uses not-menu-open: CSS variant classes on the nav element (HeaderMain.php line 130) while TSX uses React state-based conditional classes (lines 726-731). The TSX approach (default collapsed, conditionally expanded) is correct. The PHP approach should match by using default classes instead of not-menu-open: variant classes. Other structural elements are in sync.

cssAdditions.notes: CRITICAL: @custom-variant not-menu-open was RE-ADDED to states.css (line 5). The user explicitly stated this should NOT exist. Previous QA run confirmed it was removed. The developer re-added it as FIX-009. states.css now has 5 variants: open, fixed, active, menu-open, not-menu-open. It should have only 4 (without not-menu-open).

specCompliance.notes: All 6 test page variations render correctly. Container wrapper added per Fix 1. Search forms work. SVG assets render. SEP mobile pill appears only on variations with sepLink data. Scroll behavior classes vary correctly across variations.

issues.description: The @custom-variant not-menu-open was RE-ADDED to states.css. The user EXPLICITLY instructed this variant should NOT exist. The previous fix cycle removed it, and the previous QA confirmed it was gone. The developer re-added it in FIX-009. The code should use the default-state approach: elements start collapsed (max-h-0 opacity-0 pointer-events-none as default classes), and the menu-open: variant expands them (menu-open:max-h-[2000px] menu-open:opacity-100 menu-open:pointer-events-auto). Any CSS using not-menu-open: should be rewritten to use the default state instead.
issues.alsoUpdate: blocks/Header/HeaderMain/HeaderMain.php:130 -- Replace 'not-menu-open:max-md:max-h-0 not-menu-open:max-md:opacity-0 not-menu-open:max-md:pointer-events-none' with default classes 'max-md:max-h-0 max-md:opacity-0 max-md:pointer-events-none' (these are the default collapsed state, overridden by menu-open: variant)

issues.description: HeaderMain.php nav element (line 130) uses not-menu-open: variant classes for the collapsed state, while HeaderMain.tsx (lines 726-731) uses React state conditional classes. The PHP should use the default-state approach matching the TSX pattern: collapsed classes as defaults (max-md:max-h-0 max-md:opacity-0 max-md:pointer-events-none max-md:overflow-hidden), expanded classes via menu-open: variant (menu-open:max-h-[2000px] menu-open:opacity-100 menu-open:pointer-events-auto menu-open:pt-4 menu-open:pb-8).

fix.current: not-menu-open:max-md:max-h-0 not-menu-open:max-md:opacity-0 not-menu-open:max-md:pointer-events-none menu-open:max-h-[2000px] menu-open:opacity-100 menu-open:pointer-events-auto menu-open:pt-4 menu-open:pb-8
fix.suggested: max-md:max-h-0 max-md:opacity-0 max-md:pointer-events-none max-md:overflow-hidden menu-open:max-h-[2000px] menu-open:opacity-100 menu-open:pointer-events-auto menu-open:pt-4 menu-open:pb-8

issues.description: 8 prettier/prettier formatting errors in HeaderMain.tsx at lines 362, 375, 389, 562, 606, 614, 623, 630. These were introduced by FIX-006 and FIX-007 changes.
Unused 'clientId' parameter in Header.tsx line 22. The Edit function destructures clientId but does not use it. sortBlocks() on line 23 does not take clientId as argument.

fix.from: export default function Edit( { clientId }: EditProps ) {

issues.description: Two BaseControl components in the Behaviour panel (Desktop at line 435 and Mobile at line 468) have label property but no id property. WordPress lint rule @wordpress/no-base-control-with-label-without-id requires both.
Prettier formatting error in HeaderMainItem.tsx line 57 (carried over from previous run as FQA-R001). innerBlocksProps className line has a space where a newline + indentation is expected.

The search form appears twice in the PHP output (desktop utility row + mobile menu panel). This is functionally a markup duplication -- the same search form rendered in two DOM locations with CSS hiding (hidden md:flex vs md:hidden). However, forms are a more complex case than simple links since they contain inputs. The TSX does the same pattern. This is an observation for potential future improvement (single search form repositioned via CSS/JS) but is acceptable for now given the form element complexity.
issues.note: Page audit confirms count=2 search forms per section. Both TSX and PHP have this pattern. Acceptable exception due to form element complexity, but worth noting.

All spec-required attributes defined with correct types and defaults
Test page has 6 variations with real media (logoId: 6), matching spec
Container wrapper added to Header.tsx and Header.php (FIX-001 verified)
Submenu text-white class added in both TSX and PHP (FIX-002 verified)
Logo uses self-stretch **:h-full in both TSX and PHP (FIX-003 verified)
Search is now a proper <form> with <input type='search'> and submit button (FIX-004 verified)
SVG asset paths corrected -- all theme_block_asset() calls work (FIX-005 verified)
SEP renamed to 'Call to Action' throughout editor UI (FIX-006 verified)
Sidebar panel titles and labels improved (FIX-007 verified)
Mobile toggle button renders with hamburger/close SVGs (FIX-008 verified)

JS implements focus trapping, Escape key, ArrowUp/Down keyboard navigation
